rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers or declared helpers within the same annex.
Implementation-level bindings should use assign for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE scalar types must be int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
When type mismatches occur, align helper declarations with the actual expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Use only identifiers visible in the current AADL scope or declared in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers or types in AGREE contracts without prior declaration.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Component type vs implementation scope boundaries
